---
title: "Source Guide: Eigenvalues and Diagonalization"
description: "A curated source guide for learning eigenvalues and diagonalization from first pass through graph- and dynamics-facing research bridges."
page-type: source-guide
status: published
update-status: current
level: foundation
topic: linear-algebra
prerequisites:
  - /topics/foundation/linear-algebra/concepts/eigenvalues-and-diagonalization.qmd
learning-goals:
  - "Choose the right spectral source for first pass, second pass, or paper bridge."
  - "Separate durable eigenvalue references from current graph-facing bridges."
  - "See which sources belong to the concept, application, and research pages in this package."
applications:
  - graph-learning
  - dynamical-systems
keywords:
  - source guide
  - eigenvalues
  - diagonalization
  - spectral methods
maintainer: mtuann
last-reviewed: 2026-04-24
source-check-date: 2026-04-24
review-cadence: semiannual
date-modified: last-modified
draft: false
stable-sources:
  - "MIT 18.06 Lecture 21: Eigenvalues and Eigenvectors"
  - "MIT 18.06SC Lecture 22: Diagonalization and Powers of A"
  - "Hefferon, Linear Algebra"
current-sources:
  - "A Comprehensive Survey on Spectral Clustering with Graph Structure Learning (2025)"
  - "Piecewise Constant Spectral Graph Neural Network (2025)"
---

## How To Use This Guide

Use one layer at a time:

- `First pass` for the geometric and modal picture
- `Second pass` for more proofs and exercises
- `Paper bridge` for graph and spectral-learning directions

## Stable Core

- [MIT 18.06: Lecture 21, Eigenvalues and Eigenvectors](https://live.ocw.mit.edu/courses/18-06-linear-algebra-spring-2010/resources/lecture-21-eigenvalues-and-eigenvectors/) - `First pass` - best official first stop for spectral intuition and computation. Checked `2026-04-24`.
- [MIT 18.06SC: Lecture 22, Diagonalization and Powers of A](https://live.ocw.mit.edu/courses/18-06sc-linear-algebra-fall-2011/resources/lecture-22-diagonalization-and-powers-of-a-1/) - `First pass` - official source for matrix powers and diagonalization. Checked `2026-04-24`.
- [Hefferon, Linear Algebra](https://hefferon.net/linearalgebra/) - `Second pass` - stronger self-study source for proof and exercise depth. Checked `2026-04-24`.

## Current Bridge

- [A Comprehensive Survey on Spectral Clustering with Graph Structure Learning](https://arxiv.org/abs/2501.13597) - `Second pass` - current map from classical spectral modes to graph clustering with learned structure. Checked `2026-04-24`.

## Paper Bridge

- [Piecewise Constant Spectral Graph Neural Network](https://openreview.net/pdf?id=sTdVnDW0HX) - `Paper bridge` - current example of explicit spectral design in graph learning. Checked `2026-04-24`.

## Recommended Reading Paths

### Path 1: First serious understanding

1. MIT 18.06 Lecture 21
2. MIT 18.06SC Lecture 22
3. [Eigenvalues and Diagonalization](../concepts/eigenvalues-and-diagonalization.qmd)

### Path 2: Modes and dynamics

1. [Eigenvalues and Diagonalization](../concepts/eigenvalues-and-diagonalization.qmd)
2. [Spectral Modes in Consensus and Graphs](../applications/spectral-modes-in-consensus-and-graphs.qmd)
3. [Computation Lab: Matrix Powers and Spectral Modes](../labs/matrix-powers-and-spectral-modes.qmd)

### Path 3: Bridge to graph learning

1. [Eigenvalues and Diagonalization](../concepts/eigenvalues-and-diagonalization.qmd)
2. [Spectral Clustering and Graph Modes](../paper-lab/spectral-clustering-and-graph-modes.qmd)
3. [Spectral Methods from Graphs to GNNs](../research/spectral-methods-from-graphs-to-gnns.qmd)

## Sources Checked Online

All links above were checked on `2026-04-24`.

The verification pass included:

- official MIT OCW pages
- official author-maintained textbook page
- current arXiv survey page for spectral clustering
- current OpenReview page for a spectral GNN paper
